subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
lyrics:
[Verse]
Doppler bleeding green across the dash
I taped a photo of you to the windshield glass
Mile marker forty, the funnel drops its hand
I choose the cloud, I always choose the cloud, not land

[Chorus]
Spin me, spin me, supercell tonight
I'm a needle on a barometer, swinging into white
You wanted Sunday mornings, slippers by the door
But I was born to follow what the wind is reaching for

[Verse]
Motel keys and a radar app gone red
Gas station coffee, a roadmap on the bed
You said come home before the season ends
I sent a postcard from a county with no friends

[Chorus]
Spin me, spin me, supercell tonight
I'm a needle on a barometer, swinging into white
You wanted Sunday mornings, slippers by the door
But I was born to follow what the wind is reaching for

[Bridge]
Mirrorball hail on a Kansas county line
I dance with the downdraft, I call the chaos mine
Love is a porch light, steady and small
But I keep driving toward the thing that swallows it all

subject: the last tree in a paved-over town
lyrics:
[Verse]
They poured the gray around my only root
A parking line runs straight beneath my fruit
The meters hum a frequency of coins
While something old inside my heartwood joins

[Chorus]
I am the green inside the gray
The held breath the concrete couldn't take away
Wind remembers, wind remembers my name
When every other leaf became a flame of asphalt rain

[Verse]
A sparrow keeps a single morning chord
A child once carved initials, now ignored
The streetlight thinks it is the moon at night
I drink the rain that slips between the white

[Bridge]
No forest left to answer when I sway
Just echoes off the bank across the way
Still I unfold one stubborn April vein
A cathedral standing in a lot of rain

subject: a recurring dream of flying
lyrics:
[Verse]
Alarm clock screaming, ceiling pressing down
Nine to five is a leash that drags me round
But every night the gravity lets go
And I'm above the rooftops nobody owns

[Chorus]
I flew again, I flew again last night
Kicked off the pavement, cleared the streetlight light
Don't tell me how the morning's gonna land
I had the whole sky burning in my hands

[Verse]
Three minutes free before the buzzer hits
My body's chained to a desk that never quits
But I remember tilting past the wires
Laughing at the asphalt and the liars

[Chorus]
I flew again, I flew again last night
Kicked off the pavement, cleared the streetlight light
Don't tell me how the morning's gonna land
I had the whole sky burning in my hands

[Bridge]
They wake you up, they pin you to the floor
They tell you feet were made for nothing more
But I keep the runway folded in my chest
And I take off the second I shut my eyes to rest

subject: the last dance of a long marriage
lyrics:
[Verse]
Forty winters worn into these hands
Your heel still finds the rhythm where it lands
The guitar weeps a key we learned by heart
We move together now to move apart

[Chorus]
Una vez mas, one final compas
The shawl across your shoulders, the years across the glass
Clap for the silence after we are done
Two shadows turning slow against the setting sun

[Verse]
Your wedding ring, a coin worn smooth and pale
My boot heel keeps the count, my old heart frail
The room holds breath the way a church holds smoke
Every turn a vow, every stomp a word we spoke

[Bridge]
No more castanets to call the morning back
Just the tremble in the strings, the worn black track
We bow, we hold, the last note will not stay
But god, mi amor, what a long and burning way

subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
lyrics:
[Verse]
The jars were singing under burlap shrouds
My daddy's recipe and a sky of clouds
The county road wore fog like a funeral veil
And headlights came up cold behind the rail

[Chorus]
Lord, the still ran dry and the river ran red
I buried the bottle where the willows bled
The revenue man got a story to tell
And I got a curve that I remember too well

[Verse]
Gravel like teeth in the bottom of the dark
A blown front tire and the dogwood spark
The brake went soft like a promise breaks
And the corn liquor caught what the hard turn takes

[Bridge]
Somewhere a mother sets a plate for two
The second one's cold, the way these things do
I walk the ditch with the smell of fire and rye
And a moon that won't ever look me in the eye

subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
lyrics:
[Verse]
Trunk full of jars and the city lights ahead
Bassline thumping like the engine in my chest
I greased the highway, I had a getaway plan
With a mirrorball moon and a fake ID tan

[Chorus]
Run, run, the bottles gonna roll tonight
Blue and red are flashing under strobe-light white
I thought I'd dance the cargo all the way to dawn
But the law caught the rhythm and the deal was gone

[Verse]
High heels on the gas, sequins on the seat
A checkpoint shining where the off-ramp meets
I smiled like Saturday, I played it cool
But the cork popped loud and I broke the rule

[Chorus]
Run, run, the bottles gonna roll tonight
Blue and red are flashing under strobe-light white
I thought I'd dance the cargo all the way to dawn
But the law caught the rhythm and the deal was gone

[Bridge]
Glitter on the asphalt, glass across the lane
The disco ball is spinning in the cruiser's rain
I'll do my time in a beat I understand
Still clapping out the offbeat with a cuffed-up hand

subject: studying alone at two in the morning
lyrics:
[Verse]
Desk lamp humming like a tiny sun
Highlighter bleeding through page two-oh-one
The building's sleeping but my pulse won't quit
Got a dancefloor heartbeat and a textbook to hit

[Chorus]
Two in the morning, just me and the groove
Flashcards spinning, the only ones that move
Nobody's watching, so I let it ride
A one-person discotheque on the quiet side

[Verse]
Cold coffee rings make a vinyl on the wood
I conjugate the verbs the way a dancer would
The vending machine glows like a city block
And my pencil keeps the four-on-the-floor o'clock

[Chorus]
Two in the morning, just me and the groove
Flashcards spinning, the only ones that move
Nobody's watching, so I let it ride
A one-person discotheque on the quiet side

[Bridge]
Sunrise is a deadline dressed in gold
I'll learn the whole chapter before it's told
Spin the chorus softly, don't wake the hall
Me and the midnight, acing it all

subject: a moonshine run gone wrong
lyrics:
[Verse]
Loaded up the Ford 'fore the rooster crowed
Mason jars rattling down the holler road
Banjo in my mind and the dew on the pine
Carrying my granddaddy's white lightning shine

[Chorus]
Run, boys, run, but the run went wrong
The sheriff knew the words to my running song
Fiddle on the dashboard, prayer on my lips
And a busted axle where the deal slips

[Verse]
Forded the creek where the cottonwoods lean
The water took the jars and the cargo clean
Now I'm standing in the shallows counting loss
With a soaking pair of boots and a hard-won cross

[Bridge]
Mama always said don't follow that trade
But a poor man's pocket is a debt unpaid
So I'll pick my banjo and I'll tell it true
How the river drank the corn and the morning, too

subject: teaching your daughter to drive
lyrics:
[Verse]
Manos en las diez y las dos, mija
Empty parking lot, Sunday, no prisa
You kill the engine twice and you laugh out loud
Little girl gone tall, behind the wheel now proud

[Chorus]
Dale suave, suave, easy on the brake
Every green light's a future you gonna take
I'm in the passenger seat letting go, letting go
My whole heart riding shotgun, watching you grow

[Verse]
Mirror, signal, breathe, you got the flow
First time on the avenue, radio low
Your knuckles white, then your shoulders drop
And the city opens up at the four-way stop

[Chorus]
Dale suave, suave, easy on the brake
Every green light's a future you gonna take
I'm in the passenger seat letting go, letting go
My whole heart riding shotgun, watching you grow

[Bridge]
One day pretty soon you gonna drive away
Taillights down a road I cannot stay
But tonight you park it perfect by the curb
And I clap like you just conquered the whole earth, palabra

subject: calling your mother after years of silence
lyrics:
[Verse]
Thumb above the green, been hovering for days
Four years of static in a hundred different ways
The dial tone's a heartbeat I forgot the sound
Vinyl crackle morning, rain coming down

[Chorus]
Hey ma, it's me, it's been a minute, I know
The line went quiet but the love didn't go
Put the kettle on, I'm finally on the phone
A long way from sorry, still finding my way home

[Verse]
She picks up slow like she knew it all along
Same breath, same pause, the start of the same song
I count the years out loud and they don't weigh as much
When her voice still folds around me like a touch

[Bridge]
No big apology, just coffee-cup small talk
The weather, the cousins, the cat, the morning walk
And somewhere in the nothing we begin again
Two people learning how to be a we, not when

subject: two astronauts drifting apart
lyrics:
[Verse]
Tether came loose somewhere past the moon
Your voice in my helmet a half-step out of tune
The Earth's a blue coin spinning down the dark
And the distance keeps its own slow walking arc

[Chorus]
Drift, baby, drift, the silence in between
A minor key of starlight, a love we cannot lean
I reach, you reach, our gloved hands miss the bar
Two metronomes of breathing, growing far

[Verse]
Radio thins to a whisper, then a hiss
I hum the standard that we used to kiss
The oxygen counts down in lazy blue
And I improvise a goodbye made for two

[Bridge]
No gravity to hold the note in place
Just the swing of two bodies losing space
Play it soft, brushes on the snare of night
We fade to black on the downbeat of the light

subject: a storm chaser who can't settle down
lyrics:
[Verse]
I sold the truck twice and I bought it back
There's a hailstone dent for every Texas track
My wedding suit still smells of diesel rain
And the wheat goes flat when I come down the lane

[Chorus]
I can't stay, darlin', when the sky turns wrong
There's a green-black promise calling me along
You built us a kitchen, you hung us a door
But I love the open road that wants me more

[Verse]
Grain elevator ghosts in the rearview gold
A county fair radio playing something old
I chase the anvil clouds till the daylight's gone
And I sleep where the thunder writes its song

[Bridge]
Maybe when the season finally breaks
I'll learn the weight a quiet morning takes
But the wind's got my name in a low refrain
And I answer it again, and again, and again

subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
lyrics:
[Verse]
Gold lights swinging on the reception floor
Never met you 'fore the DJ opened the door
You caught my rhythm in the chorus of the night
Two strangers spinning in the auntie spotlight

[Chorus]
Wine for me, baby, move your waist so slow
Don't know your name but the body already know
The drum say closer, the bass say stay
We dance like the morning is a lifetime away

[Verse]
The couple cuts the cake, but we cut the air
Confetti in your braids, jollof in the air
You laugh and the speakers catch the sound
We own a little circle of the holy ground

[Chorus]
Wine for me, baby, move your waist so slow
Don't know your name but the body already know
The drum say closer, the bass say stay
We dance like the morning is a lifetime away

[Bridge]
Maybe we forget by the morning bus
Maybe this whole wedding was a song for us
But for three minutes under the disco palm
You were my wedding, you were my calm

subject: a chess hustler in the park
lyrics:
[Verse]
Five bucks a game on a fold-out card
Pigeons keep the time in the courthouse yard
My clock got a crack and my king got grit
Sit down, young blood, let me lighten your kit

[Chorus]
Knight to the corner, take my queen if you dare
I been losing on purpose since you sat in that chair
The city walks by but it don't understand
There's a whole working life in the back of my hand

[Verse]
Winter in my gloves with the fingertips gone
I been playing this square since the streetlights came
A banker, a busker, a kid skipping school
They all learn the price of a confident fool

[Bridge]
They tore down the diner, they raised up the rent
But the board's still free and the hours well spent
Checkmate, friend, now ante up again
The park is my office and the pawns are my pen

subject: running a pirate radio station
lyrics:
[Verse]
Antenna lashed to a water tower's bone
Midnight hum where the signal lives alone
I press the key and the static parts like sea
This is nowhere station, broadcasting to be

[Chorus]
Can you hear me out there past the licensed light
A frequency of strangers in the open night
No call sign, no permission, just a tone
A lantern made of airwaves for the ones alone

[Verse]
A trucker on the interstate, a sleepless nurse
We share a borrowed channel of the universe
I spin the records nobody else will play
And let the dead air breathe till the break of gray

[Bridge]
They triangulate the towers, close the door
I move the rig to a rooftop one block more
As long as one receiver glows in the dark
The pirate keeps the flame, the ghost keeps the spark
